name: Visit Timepoint Assignment
shortDescription: Convert longitudinal visit dates into study timepoints (T1, T2, ...) using CBR TLSA/SANSCOG window rules.
description: Takes a longitudinal cohort table with one row per visit and assigns each visit a study timepoint. For every participant the earliest visit becomes the baseline; each later visit's elapsed time from baseline is matched against the cohort's timepoint window schedule (TLSA: annual, SANSCOG: biennial). When several visits fall in the same window, one is kept according to the chosen rule and the collision is reported. Emits the timepointed table plus a duplicate-visit QC report.
inputDescription: One tabular file (parquet, csv or xlsx) with one row per visit. Must contain a participant identifier column and a visit date column.

name.label: Name
name.description: Unique identifier for this node in the pipeline

scheme.label: Timepoint schedule
scheme.description: Visit window schedule to apply. TLSA follows the annual schedule and SANSCOG the biennial schedule from the CBR protocol. Choose Custom to enter your own window bounds below.

participant_column.label: Participant ID column
participant_column.description: Column identifying the participant. Baseline and timepoints are computed independently within each participant.

date_column.label: Visit date column
date_column.description: Column holding the visit date. Rows with an unparseable or missing date are dropped and counted in the log.

cohort_column.label: Cohort column
cohort_column.description: Optional. If set together with Cohort value, rows are filtered to that cohort before assignment — this replaces a separate split step.

cohort_value.label: Cohort value
cohort_value.description: Optional. Keep only rows whose cohort column equals this value (e.g. TLSA or SANSCOG). Ignored if Cohort column is blank.

collision_rule.label: When two visits land in one window
collision_rule.description: Which visit to keep when a participant has more than one visit inside the same timepoint window. Applies from T2 onward — T1 is always the participant's earliest visit, since that is what defines the baseline. Every collision is reported in the duplicates output regardless of this setting.

options.label: Keep the visit closest to the ideal window centre

promote_late_baseline.label: Promote a late baseline visit to T2
promote_late_baseline.description: If a participant has no visit in the T2 window but has a late T1 visit near the T2 boundary, relabel that visit as T2. Only applies when the participant has a separate, earlier visit still serving as T1.

promote_window_fraction.label: Late-baseline promotion band
promote_window_fraction.description: How close to the T1/T2 boundary a visit must be to qualify for promotion, as a fraction of the T1 window. 0.95 means the last 5% of the T1 window. Only used when promotion is enabled.

days_per_year.label: Days per year
days_per_year.description: Divisor used to convert elapsed days into years. Use 365.25 to account for leap years, or 365 to reproduce the original analysis exactly.

max_timepoints.label: Maximum timepoints
max_timepoints.description: Upper bound on how many timepoints to search for. Visits beyond the last window are left unassigned and reported in the log.

custom_windows.label: Custom window definition
custom_windows.description: Only used when Timepoint schedule is Custom. Object with keys: interval_years, t1_max, t2_min, t2_max, lower_margin, upper_margin. Timepoint n (n>=3) covers [(n-1)*interval - lower_margin, (n-1)*interval + upper_margin].

drop_unassigned.label: Drop unassigned visits
drop_unassigned.description: Remove visits that fall outside every window and losing visits from a collision. Leave off to keep them with a blank timepoint so nothing is silently lost.

outputDescription: Timepointed visit table, plus a QC report of visits that collided within a window.
